disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A pre-existing naevus has evolved rapidly over ~8 weeks — increased in diameter (roughly doubled), become elevated, and changed/darkened in colour.
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me how the spot has changed over time?
Was this mole always like this, or has it changed recently?
How quickly has it been changing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It used to be a small flat brown freckle for years. Over the last two months or so it's got noticeably bigger, it's raised up now, and the colour has gone darker and blotchy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Spontaneous bleeding from the pigmented lesion today, preceded by new localised itch, in a lesion that has never bled before.
trigger_question_examples: Tell me about the bleeding — what happened today?
Has the spot ever bled before today?
Did anything happen just before it started bleeding?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's never bled before. Today it got itchy and then it just started oozing a bit of blood on its own onto my shirt. That's what scared me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lesion looks distinctly different from the patient's other naevi — the 'ugly duckling' sign.
trigger_question_examples: Compared with your other moles and freckles, does this one look different?
Does this spot stand out from the rest of your moles?
Do your other moles look like this one, or is this one the odd one out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you say it — yeah, my wife keeps saying this one looks nothing like my other moles. The others are all plain and similar; this one is the odd one out.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I've got a few moles, I haven't really compared them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lesion has an irregular, notched border and multiple colours (black, brown, grey with a reddish periphery), with an elevated centre.
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the shape and edges of the spot?
What colours can you see in it — is it one even colour or several?
Is the border smooth and round, or uneven?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The edge is all uneven and jagged, not a neat circle. And it's not one colour — there's black and brown and a greyish bit, with a red edge. The middle is bumped up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Significant risk factor: repeated severe sunburns in childhood/youth and high cumulative sun exposure.
trigger_question_examples: Have you had a lot of sun exposure or bad sunburns over the years?
Did you get sunburnt much when you were younger?
Do you work or spend a lot of time outdoors?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I was outside all the time as a kid and got burnt to a blister more than once. Never was much good at sunscreen back then.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No prior skin cancers, no anticoagulant medication, and systemically well (no weight loss, no lumps noticed) — pertinent negatives.
trigger_question_examples: Have you ever had a skin cancer or a spot cut out before?
Are you on any blood-thinning medication?
Have you noticed any lumps, weight loss, or feeling generally unwell?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, never had anything cut out before. I'm not on any blood thinners or any regular tablets. I feel well otherwise — no lumps or weight loss that I've noticed.
